first-meaningful-paint.title: First Meaningful Paint
first-meaningful-paint.description: First Meaningful Paint measures when the primary content of a page is visible. [Learn more](https://web.dev/first-meaningful-paint/).

total-byte-weight.title: Avoid enormous network payloads
total-byte-weight.description: Large network payloads cost users real money and are highly correlated with long load times. [Learn more](https://web.dev/total-byte-weight/).

resource-summary.title: Keep request counts low and transfer sizes small
resource-summary.description: To set budgets for the quantity and size of page resources, add a budget.json file. [Learn more](https://web.dev/use-lighthouse-for-performance-budgets/).

lighthouseRawResult.userAgent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) HeadlessChrome/126.0.6478.56 Safari/537.36

environment.hostUserAgent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) HeadlessChrome/126.0.6478.56 Safari/537.36

is-on-https.title: Does not use HTTPS
is-on-https.description: All sites should be protected with HTTPS, even ones that don't handle sensitive data. This includes avoiding [mixed content](https://developers.google.com/web/fundamentals/security/prevent-mixed-content/what-is-mixed-content), where some resources are loaded over HTTP despite the initial request being served over HTTPS. HTTPS prevents intruders from tampering with or passively listening in on the communications between your app and your users, and is a prerequisite for HTTP/2 and many new web platform APIs. [Learn more](https://web.dev/is-on-https/).

service-worker.title: Does not register a service worker that controls page and `start_url`
service-worker.description: The service worker is the technology that enables your app to use many Progressive Web App features, such as offline, add to homescreen, and push notifications. [Learn more](https://web.dev/service-worker/).

viewport.title: Does not have a `<meta name="viewport">` tag with `width` or `initial-scale`
viewport.description: A `<meta name="viewport">` not only optimizes your app for mobile screen sizes, but also prevents [a 300 millisecond delay to user input](https://developers.google.com/web/updates/2013/12/300ms-tap-delay-gone-away). [Learn more](https://web.dev/viewport/).

first-contentful-paint.title: First Contentful Paint
first-contentful-paint.description: First Contentful Paint marks the time at which the first text or image is painted. [Learn more](https://web.dev/first-contentful-paint/).

largest-contentful-paint.title: Largest Contentful Paint
largest-contentful-paint.description: Largest Contentful Paint marks the time at which the largest text or image is painted. [Learn more](https://web.dev/lighthouse-largest-contentful-paint/)

first-meaningful-paint.title: First Meaningful Paint
first-meaningful-paint.description: First Meaningful Paint measures when the primary content of a page is visible. [Learn more](https://web.dev/first-meaningful-paint/).

speed-index.title: Speed Index
speed-index.description: Speed Index shows how quickly the contents of a page are visibly populated. [Learn more](https://web.dev/speed-index/).

total-blocking-time.title: Total Blocking Time
total-blocking-time.description: Sum of all time periods between FCP and Time to Interactive, when task length exceeded 50ms, expressed in milliseconds. [Learn more](https://web.dev/lighthouse-total-blocking-time/).

max-potential-fid.title: Max Potential First Input Delay
max-potential-fid.description: The maximum potential First Input Delay that your users could experience is the duration of the longest task. [Learn more](https://web.dev/lighthouse-max-potential-fid/).

cumulative-layout-shift.title: Cumulative Layout Shift
cumulative-layout-shift.description: Cumulative Layout Shift measures the movement of visible elements within the viewport. [Learn more](https://web.dev/cls/).

errors-in-console.title: No browser errors logged to the console
errors-in-console.description: Errors logged to the console indicate unresolved problems. They can come from network request failures and other browser concerns. [Learn more](https://web.dev/errors-in-console/)

server-response-time.title: Reduce initial server response time
server-response-time.description: Keep the server response time for the main document short because all other requests depend on it. [Learn more](https://web.dev/time-to-first-byte/).

interactive.title: Time to Interactive
interactive.description: Time to interactive is the amount of time it takes for the page to become fully interactive. [Learn more](https://web.dev/interactive/).

user-timings.title: User Timing marks and measures
user-timings.description: Consider instrumenting your app with the User Timing API to measure your app's real-world performance during key user experiences. [Learn more](https://web.dev/user-timings/).

critical-request-chains.title: Avoid chaining critical requests
critical-request-chains.description: The Critical Request Chains below show you what resources are loaded with a high priority. Consider reducing the length of chains, reducing the download size of resources, or deferring the download of unnecessary resources to improve page load. [Learn more](https://web.dev/critical-request-chains/).

redirects.title: Avoid multiple page redirects
redirects.description: Redirects introduce additional delays before the page can be loaded. [Learn more](https://web.dev/redirects/).

installable-manifest.title: Web app manifest and service worker meet the installability requirements
installable-manifest.description: Service worker is the technology that enables your app to use many Progressive Web App features, such as offline, add to homescreen, and push notifications. With proper service worker and manifest implementations, browsers can proactively prompt users to add your app to their homescreen, which can lead to higher engagement. [Learn more](https://web.dev/installable-manifest/).

apple-touch-icon.title: Does not provide a valid `apple-touch-icon`
apple-touch-icon.description: For ideal appearance on iOS when users add a progressive web app to the home screen, define an `apple-touch-icon`. It must point to a non-transparent 192px (or 180px) square PNG. [Learn More](https://web.dev/apple-touch-icon/).

splash-screen.title: Is not configured for a custom splash screen
splash-screen.description: A themed splash screen ensures a high-quality experience when users launch your app from their homescreens. [Learn more](https://web.dev/splash-screen/).
splash-screen.explanation:
Failures: Manifest does not have a PNG icon of at least 512px,
Manifest does not have `background_color`,
Manifest does not have `theme_color`,
Manifest does not have `name`.

failures: Manifest does not have a PNG icon of at least 512px

themed-omnibox.title: Does not set a theme color for the address bar.
themed-omnibox.description: The browser address bar can be themed to match your site. [Learn more](https://web.dev/themed-omnibox/).
themed-omnibox.explanation:
Failures: Manifest does not have `theme_color`,
No `<meta name="theme-color">` tag found.

maskable-icon.title: Manifest doesn't have a maskable icon
maskable-icon.description: A maskable icon ensures that the image fills the entire shape without being letterboxed when installing the app on a device. [Learn more](https://web.dev/maskable-icon-audit/).

content-width.title: Content is not sized correctly for the viewport
content-width.description: If the width of your app's content doesn't match the width of the viewport, your app might not be optimized for mobile screens. [Learn more](https://web.dev/content-width/).
content-width.explanation: The viewport size of 980px does not match the window size of 360px.

image-aspect-ratio.title: Displays images with correct aspect ratio
image-aspect-ratio.description: Image display dimensions should match natural aspect ratio. [Learn more](https://web.dev/image-aspect-ratio/).

image-size-responsive.title: Serves images with low resolution
image-size-responsive.description: Image natural dimensions should be proportional to the display size and the pixel ratio to maximize image clarity. [Learn more](https://web.dev/serve-responsive-images/).

preload-fonts.title: Fonts with `font-display: optional` are preloaded
preload-fonts.description: Preload `optional` fonts so first-time visitors may use them. [Learn more](https://web.dev/preload-optional-fonts/)

deprecations.title: Uses deprecated APIs
deprecations.description: Deprecated APIs will eventually be removed from the browser. [Learn more](https://web.dev/deprecations/).

mainthread-work-breakdown.title: Minimizes main-thread work
mainthread-work-breakdown.description: Consider reducing the time spent parsing, compiling and executing JS. You may find delivering smaller JS payloads helps with this. [Learn more](https://web.dev/mainthread-work-breakdown/)

bootup-time.title: JavaScript execution time
bootup-time.description: Consider reducing the time spent parsing, compiling, and executing JS. You may find delivering smaller JS payloads helps with this. [Learn more](https://web.dev/bootup-time/).

uses-rel-preload.title: Preload key requests
uses-rel-preload.description: Consider using `<link rel=preload>` to prioritize fetching resources that are currently requested later in page load. [Learn more](https://web.dev/uses-rel-preload/).

uses-rel-preconnect.title: Preconnect to required origins
uses-rel-preconnect.description: Consider adding `preconnect` or `dns-prefetch` resource hints to establish early connections to important third-party origins. [Learn more](https://web.dev/uses-rel-preconnect/).

font-display.title: All text remains visible during webfont loads
font-display.description: Leverage the font-display CSS feature to ensure text is user-visible while webfonts are loading. [Learn more](https://web.dev/font-display/).

network-requests.title: Network Requests
network-requests.description: Lists the network requests that were made during page load.

network-rtt.title: Network Round Trip Times
network-rtt.description: Network round trip times (RTT) have a large impact on performance. If the RTT to an origin is high, it's an indication that servers closer to the user could improve performance. [Learn more](https://hpbn.co/primer-on-latency-and-bandwidth/).

network-server-latency.title: Server Backend Latencies
network-server-latency.description: Server latencies can impact web performance. If the server latency of an origin is high, it's an indication the server is overloaded or has poor backend performance. [Learn more](https://hpbn.co/primer-on-web-performance/#analyzing-the-resource-waterfall).

main-thread-tasks.title: Tasks
main-thread-tasks.description: Lists the toplevel main thread tasks that executed during page load.

performance-budget.title: Performance budget
performance-budget.description: Keep the quantity and size of network requests under the targets set by the provided performance budget. [Learn more](https://developers.google.com/web/tools/lighthouse/audits/budgets).

timing-budget.title: Timing budget
timing-budget.description: Set a timing budget to help you keep an eye on the performance of your site. Performant sites load fast and respond to user input events quickly. [Learn more](https://developers.google.com/web/tools/lighthouse/audits/budgets).

resource-summary.title: Keep request counts low and transfer sizes small
resource-summary.description: To set budgets for the quantity and size of page resources, add a budget.json file. [Learn more](https://web.dev/use-lighthouse-for-performance-budgets/).

third-party-summary.title: Minimize third-party usage
third-party-summary.description: Third-party code can significantly impact load performance. Limit the number of redundant third-party providers and try to load third-party code after your page has primarily finished loading. [Learn more](https://developers.google.com/web/fundamentals/performance/optimizing-content-efficiency/loading-third-party-javascript/).

third-party-facades.title: Lazy load third-party resources with facades
third-party-facades.description: Some third-party embeds can be lazy loaded. Consider replacing them with a facade until they are required. [Learn more](https://web.dev/third-party-facades/).

largest-contentful-paint-element.title: Largest Contentful Paint element
largest-contentful-paint-element.description: This is the largest contentful element painted within the viewport. [Learn More](https://web.dev/lighthouse-largest-contentful-paint/)

lcp-lazy-loaded.title: Largest Contentful Paint image was not lazily loaded
lcp-lazy-loaded.description: Above-the-fold images that are lazily loaded render later in the page lifecycle, which can delay the largest contentful paint. [Learn more](https://web.dev/lcp-lazy-loading/).

layout-shift-elements.title: Avoid large layout shifts
layout-shift-elements.description: These DOM elements contribute most to the CLS of the page.

Si riunisce il Consiglio Comunale lunedì 24 giugno 2024
Pubblicato Giovedì, 20 Giugno 2024 16:21

Viene resa nota la convocazione del Consiglio Comunale in seduta straordinaria lunedì 24 giugno 2024 alle ore 17:00. I punti all’ordine del giorno sono:

• convalida degli eletti nelle consultazioni elettorali del 8 e 9 giugno 2024: esame delle condizioni di eleggibilità e compatibilità alla carica di Sindaco e di Consigliere comunale (art. 41 D.lgs. 267/2000);
• giuramento del Sindaco, art. 50, co. 11, D.lgs. 267/2000;
• comunicazione nomina Vice Sindaco e componenti Giunta comunale;
• comunicazione istituzione gruppi consiliari e individuazione capigruppo;
• elezione commissione elettorale comunale;
• definizione degli indirizzi per la nomina e la designazione dei rappresentanti del Comune e del Consiglio presso enti, aziende ed istituzioni;
• nomina commissione per la formazione degli elenchi dei giudici popolari;
• determinazione aggiornamento biennale del piano economico finanziario (PEF) 2024-2025;
• approvazione piano tariffario TARI per l'anno d'imposta 2024;
• variazione al Bilancio di previsione esercizio finanziario 2024/2026 - Ratifica Delibera di G.C. n. 58 del 31/05/2024 - Adottata ai sensi dell'art. 175 c. 4, D.lgs. 267/2000;
• comunicazione deliberazione n. 145/2024/VSG adottata dalla Corte dei conti - Sezione regionale di controllo per l'Abruzzo in merito alla relazione di fine mandato 2019-2024.

Avviso pubblico per la nomina di assessore donna
Pubblicato Lunedì, 17 Giugno 2024 13:23

Si rende noto l'avviso pubblico per la presentazione di candidature per la nomina di una persona di sesso femminile per ricoprire il ruolo dì asscssore comunale esterno.

Le candidature possono essere inoltrate a mezzo PEC all'indirizzo info@pcc.comune.orsogna.chieti.it o presentate direttamente all'Ufficio Protocollo entro le ore 12:00 del giorno 20 giugno 2024, allegando il proprio curriculum e una dichiarazione sostitutiva in merito al possesso dei requisiti sopra indicati e all'accettazione del programma amministrativo e delle linee programmatiche di questa Amministrazione comunale.

Documentazione allegata:

- Avviso pubblico
- Modulo di domanda

Colonia marina estiva ragazzi
Pubblicato Martedì, 11 Giugno 2024 19:21

Il Comune di Orsogna organizza la colonia marina estiva per l’anno 2024 presso il Lido Saraceni di Ortona, riservata ai ragazzi che hanno frequentato la scuola primaria e secondaria di 1° grado nell’anno scolastico 2023/2024 nel comune di Orsogna, articolata in tre turni e precisamente:

- turno a dal 01 luglio al 05 luglio (sabato e domenica escluse);

- turno b dal 08 luglio al 12 luglio (sabato e domenica escluse);

- turno c dal 15 luglio al 19 luglio (sabato e domenica escluse).

Gli interessati possono presentare apposita istanza entro e non oltre le ore 12:00 di giovedì 27 giugno 2024. La quota di partecipazione alla colonia marina per ciascun turno (1 settimana) è di € 30,00 per un figlio - € 50,00 per due figli - €60,00 per tre figli. Il versamento dovrà essere effettuato mediante bollettino di c.c.p. n. 12510665 intestato al Comune di Orsogna - Servizio di tesoreria comunale, oppure con bonifico su IBAN IT70u0760103200001048608127.

Allegati:
Colonia-estiva-minori-2024.pdf	[ ]	415 Kb
Colonia-estiva-minori-2024-Domanda.pdf	[ ]	271 Kb

Si riunisce il Consiglio Comunale giovedì 2 maggio 2024
Pubblicato Venerdì, 26 Aprile 2024 22:36

Viene resa nota la convocazione del Consiglio Comunale in seduta ordinaria giovedì 2 maggio 2024 alle ore 15:00. I punti all’ordine del giorno sono:

• lettura ed approvazione verbali sedute precedenti;
• approvazione del Rendiconto della gestione per l'esercizio 2023 ai sensi dell'art. 224 D.lgs n. 267/2000;
• variazione al Bilancio di previsione esercizio finanziario 2024/2026 - Ratifica Delibera di G.C n. 39 del 15/04/2024 adottata ai sensi dell'art. 175 c. 4 D.lgs. 267/2000;
• variazione al Bilancio di previsione esercizio finanziario 2024/2026 - Ratifica Delibera di G.C. n. 42 del 23/04/2024 adottata ai sensi dell'art. 175 c. 4 D.lgs. 267/2000;
• variazione al Bilancio di previsione 2024/2026 ed applicazione dell'avanzo di amministrazione accertato sulla base del Rendiconto dell'esercizio 2023;
• variazione al Bilancio di previsione finanziario 2024/2026 (art. 175, comma 2, del D.lgs. n. 267/2000).

Si riunisce il Consiglio Comunale lunedì 22 aprile 2024
Pubblicato Lunedì, 22 Aprile 2024 11:25

Viene resa nota la convocazione del Consiglio Comunale in seduta straordinaria lunedì 22 aprile 2024 alle ore 15:00. I punti all’ordine del giorno sono:

• lettura ed approvazione verbali sedute precedenti;
• P.R.G.. Sistema insediativo urbano. Zona D - Insediamenti esistenti e di nuovo impianto di tipo produttivo - "Sottozona D1". Attivazione Comparti D1-1 e D1-4.Provvedimenti;
• Realizzazione di un fabbricato da adibire a "Casa funeraria e struttura per il commiato" in Orsogna (CH), Località Malverno. Ditta: Onoranze Funebri Masciangelo s.r.l. con Sede Legale in Via Adriatico, 23, Orsogna (CH). Procedimento previsto dall'art.8 del D.P.R. 07.09.2010, n.160, nel testo in vigore. Approvazione variante puntuale allo strumento urbanistico tramite Conferenza dei Servizi, disciplinata dagli articoli da 14 a 14-quinquies della Legge 07.08.1990, n.241, nel testo in vigore.

Relazione di fine mandato periodo 2019-2024
Pubblicato Mercoledì, 27 Marzo 2024 17:05

Viene pubblicata di seguito la Relazione di fine mandato per il periodo 2019-2024, sottoscritta dal Sindaco Ernesto Salerni, corredata dalla certificazione del Revisore dei Conti (inviata in data odierna alla Sezione regionale di controllo per l'Abruzzo della Corte dei conti). La Relazione di fine mandato è redatta ai sensi dell’articolo 4 del decreto legislativo 6 settembre 2011, n.149, recante: “Meccanismi sanzionatori e premiali relativi a regioni, province e comuni, a norma degli articoli 2, 17 e 26 della legge 5 maggio 2009, n. 42”, per descrivere le principali attività normative e amministrative svolte durante il mandato.

Documentazione allegata: Relazione di fine mandato per il periodo 2019-2024

Programma “Io studio”, erogazione borse di studio
Pubblicato Mercoledì, 27 Marzo 2024 16:39

Si porta a conoscenza della cittadinanza che, al fine di contrastare il fenomeno della dispersione scolastica, nell’ambito del Programma “Io studio” istituito dal Ministero dell’Istruzione, dell’Università e della Ricerca il “Fondo unico per il welfare dello studente e per il diritto allo studio”, la Regione Abruzzo, con Determinazione n. 52/DPG021 del 05/03/2024, ha provveduto ad approvare gli indirizzi operativi per la formazione degli elenchi dei potenziali aventi diritto ad accedere a borse di studio per l’anno scolastico 2023/2024 indirizzate a studenti delle scuole secondarie di secondo grado per l’acquisto di libri di testo, mobilità e trasporto, nonché per l’accesso a beni e servizi di natura culturale.

Lo studente dovrà rispettare i seguenti requisiti:

- residente in Abruzzo;

- iscritto/a nell’anno scolastico 2023/2024 e frequentante alla data di presentazione della domanda un istituto della scuola secondaria di secondo grado del sistema nazionale di istruzione (SIDI);

- appartenente ad un nucleo familiare con ISEE 2024 da € 0,00 a € 10.000,00.

La domanda dovrà essere presentata, corredata dalla documentazione di cui sopra, al protocollo di quest’Ente o anche a mezzo mail all’indirizzo amministrativo@comune.orsogna.chieti.it, entro e non oltre il 19 aprile 2024.




Documentazione allegata:

- Avviso di rettifica e proroga termini

- Avviso pubblico

- Richiesta beneficio

long-tasks.title: Avoid long main-thread tasks
long-tasks.description: Lists the longest tasks on the main thread, useful for identifying worst contributors to input delay. [Learn more](https://web.dev/long-tasks-devtools/)

no-unload-listeners.title: Registers an `unload` listener
no-unload-listeners.description: The `unload` event does not fire reliably and listening for it can prevent browser optimizations like the Back-Forward Cache. Use `pagehide` or `visibilitychange` events instead. [Learn more](https://web.dev/bfcache/#never-use-the-unload-event)

non-composited-animations.title: Avoid non-composited animations
non-composited-animations.description: Animations which are not composited can be janky and increase CLS. [Learn more](https://web.dev/non-composited-animations)

unsized-images.title: Image elements do not have explicit `width` and `height`
unsized-images.description: Set an explicit width and height on image elements to reduce layout shifts and improve CLS. [Learn more](https://web.dev/optimize-cls/#images-without-dimensions)

node.nodeLabel: Orsogna onora quattro ebrei trucidati nel 1944

valid-source-maps.title: Page has valid source maps
valid-source-maps.description: Source maps translate minified code to the original source code. This helps developers debug in production. In addition, Lighthouse is able to provide further insights. Consider deploying source maps to take advantage of these benefits. [Learn more](https://developers.google.com/web/tools/chrome-devtools/javascript/source-maps).

preload-lcp-image.title: Preload Largest Contentful Paint image
preload-lcp-image.description: Preload the image used by the LCP element in order to improve your LCP time. [Learn more](https://web.dev/optimize-lcp/#preload-important-resources).

csp-xss.title: Ensure CSP is effective against XSS attacks
csp-xss.description: A strong Content Security Policy (CSP) significantly reduces the risk of cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks. [Learn more](https://web.dev/csp-xss/)

items.description: No CSP found in enforcement mode

pwa-cross-browser.title: Site works cross-browser
pwa-cross-browser.description: To reach the most number of users, sites should work across every major browser. [Learn more](https://web.dev/pwa-cross-browser/).

pwa-page-transitions.title: Page transitions don't feel like they block on the network
pwa-page-transitions.description: Transitions should feel snappy as you tap around, even on a slow network. This experience is key to a user's perception of performance. [Learn more](https://web.dev/pwa-page-transitions/).

pwa-each-page-has-url.title: Each page has a URL
pwa-each-page-has-url.description: Ensure individual pages are deep linkable via URL and that URLs are unique for the purpose of shareability on social media. [Learn more](https://web.dev/pwa-each-page-has-url/).

accesskeys.title: `[accesskey]` values are unique
accesskeys.description: Access keys let users quickly focus a part of the page. For proper navigation, each access key must be unique. [Learn more](https://web.dev/accesskeys/).

aria-allowed-attr.title: `[aria-*]` attributes match their roles
aria-allowed-attr.description: Each ARIA `role` supports a specific subset of `aria-*` attributes. Mismatching these invalidates the `aria-*` attributes. [Learn more](https://web.dev/aria-allowed-attr/).

aria-command-name.title: `button`, `link`, and `menuitem` elements have accessible names
aria-command-name.description: When an element doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it with a generic name, making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. [Learn more](https://web.dev/aria-name/).

aria-hidden-body.title: `[aria-hidden="true"]` is not present on the document `<body>`
aria-hidden-body.description: Assistive technologies, like screen readers, work inconsistently when `aria-hidden="true"` is set on the document `<body>`. [Learn more](https://web.dev/aria-hidden-body/).

aria-hidden-focus.title: `[aria-hidden="true"]` elements do not contain focusable descendents
aria-hidden-focus.description: Focusable descendents within an `[aria-hidden="true"]` element prevent those interactive elements from being available to users of assistive technologies like screen readers. [Learn more](https://web.dev/aria-hidden-focus/).

aria-input-field-name.title: ARIA input fields have accessible names
aria-input-field-name.description: When an input field doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it with a generic name, making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. [Learn more](https://web.dev/aria-name/).

aria-meter-name.title: ARIA `meter` elements have accessible names
aria-meter-name.description: When an element doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it with a generic name, making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. [Learn more](https://web.dev/aria-name/).

aria-progressbar-name.title: ARIA `progressbar` elements have accessible names
aria-progressbar-name.description: When a `progressbar` element doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it with a generic name, making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. [Learn more](https://web.dev/aria-name/).

aria-required-attr.title: `[role]`s have all required `[aria-*]` attributes
aria-required-attr.description: Some ARIA roles have required attributes that describe the state of the element to screen readers. [Learn more](https://web.dev/aria-required-attr/).

aria-required-children.title: Elements with an ARIA `[role]` that require children to contain a specific `[role]` have all required children.
aria-required-children.description: Some ARIA parent roles must contain specific child roles to perform their intended accessibility functions. [Learn more](https://web.dev/aria-required-children/).

aria-required-parent.title: `[role]`s are contained by their required parent element
aria-required-parent.description: Some ARIA child roles must be contained by specific parent roles to properly perform their intended accessibility functions. [Learn more](https://web.dev/aria-required-parent/).

aria-roles.title: `[role]` values are valid
aria-roles.description: ARIA roles must have valid values in order to perform their intended accessibility functions. [Learn more](https://web.dev/aria-roles/).

aria-toggle-field-name.title: ARIA toggle fields have accessible names
aria-toggle-field-name.description: When a toggle field doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it with a generic name, making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. [Learn more](https://web.dev/aria-name/).

aria-tooltip-name.title: ARIA `tooltip` elements have accessible names
aria-tooltip-name.description: When an element doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it with a generic name, making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. [Learn more](https://web.dev/aria-name/).

aria-treeitem-name.title: ARIA `treeitem` elements have accessible names
aria-treeitem-name.description: When an element doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it with a generic name, making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. [Learn more](https://web.dev/aria-name/).

aria-valid-attr-value.title: `[aria-*]` attributes have valid values
aria-valid-attr-value.description: Assistive technologies, like screen readers, can't interpret ARIA attributes with invalid values. [Learn more](https://web.dev/aria-valid-attr-value/).

aria-valid-attr.title: `[aria-*]` attributes are valid and not misspelled
aria-valid-attr.description: Assistive technologies, like screen readers, can't interpret ARIA attributes with invalid names. [Learn more](https://web.dev/aria-valid-attr/).

button-name.title: Buttons have an accessible name
button-name.description: When a button doesn't have an accessible name, screen readers announce it as "button", making it unusable for users who rely on screen readers. [Learn more](https://web.dev/button-name/).

bypass.title: The page contains a heading, skip link, or landmark region
bypass.description: Adding ways to bypass repetitive content lets keyboard users navigate the page more efficiently. [Learn more](https://web.dev/bypass/).

color-contrast.title: Background and foreground colors have a sufficient contrast ratio
color-contrast.description: Low-contrast text is difficult or impossible for many users to read. [Learn more](https://web.dev/color-contrast/).

definition-list.title: `<dl>`'s contain only properly-ordered `<dt>` and `<dd>` groups, `<script>`, `<template>` or `<div>` elements.
definition-list.description: When definition lists are not properly marked up, screen readers may produce confusing or inaccurate output. [Learn more](https://web.dev/definition-list/).

dlitem.title: Definition list items are wrapped in `<dl>` elements
dlitem.description: Definition list items (`<dt>` and `<dd>`) must be wrapped in a parent `<dl>` element to ensure that screen readers can properly announce them. [Learn more](https://web.dev/dlitem/).

document-title.title: Document has a `<title>` element
document-title.description: The title gives screen reader users an overview of the page, and search engine users rely on it heavily to determine if a page is relevant to their search. [Learn more](https://web.dev/document-title/).

duplicate-id-active.title: `[id]` attributes on active, focusable elements are unique
duplicate-id-active.description: All focusable elements must have a unique `id` to ensure that they're visible to assistive technologies. [Learn more](https://web.dev/duplicate-id-active/).

duplicate-id-aria.title: ARIA IDs are unique
duplicate-id-aria.description: The value of an ARIA ID must be unique to prevent other instances from being overlooked by assistive technologies. [Learn more](https://web.dev/duplicate-id-aria/).

form-field-multiple-labels.title: No form fields have multiple labels
form-field-multiple-labels.description: Form fields with multiple labels can be confusingly announced by assistive technologies like screen readers which use either the first, the last, or all of the labels. [Learn more](https://web.dev/form-field-multiple-labels/).

frame-title.title: `<frame>` or `<iframe>` elements have a title
frame-title.description: Screen reader users rely on frame titles to describe the contents of frames. [Learn more](https://web.dev/frame-title/).

heading-order.title: Heading elements are not in a sequentially-descending order
heading-order.description: Properly ordered headings that do not skip levels convey the semantic structure of the page, making it easier to navigate and understand when using assistive technologies. [Learn more](https://web.dev/heading-order/).

node.explanation:
Fix any of the following:
  Heading order invalid

html-has-lang.title: `<html>` element has a `[lang]` attribute
html-has-lang.description: If a page doesn't specify a lang attribute, a screen reader assumes that the page is in the default language that the user chose when setting up the screen reader. If the page isn't actually in the default language, then the screen reader might not announce the page's text correctly. [Learn more](https://web.dev/html-has-lang/).

html-lang-valid.title: `<html>` element has a valid value for its `[lang]` attribute
html-lang-valid.description: Specifying a valid [BCP 47 language](https://www.w3.org/International/questions/qa-choosing-language-tags#question) helps screen readers announce text properly. [Learn more](https://web.dev/html-lang-valid/).

image-alt.title: Image elements do not have `[alt]` attributes
image-alt.description: Informative elements should aim for short, descriptive alternate text. Decorative elements can be ignored with an empty alt attribute. [Learn more](https://web.dev/image-alt/).

node.explanation:
Fix any of the following:
  Element does not have an alt attribute
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element has no title attribute
  Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

input-image-alt.title: `<input type="image">` elements have `[alt]` text
input-image-alt.description: When an image is being used as an `<input>` button, providing alternative text can help screen reader users understand the purpose of the button. [Learn more](https://web.dev/input-image-alt/).

label.title: Form elements have associated labels
label.description: Labels ensure that form controls are announced properly by assistive technologies, like screen readers. [Learn more](https://web.dev/label/).

link-name.title: Links do not have a discernible name
link-name.description: Link text (and alternate text for images, when used as links) that is discernible, unique, and focusable improves the navigation experience for screen reader users. [Learn more](https://web.dev/link-name/).

list.title: Lists contain only `<li>` elements and script supporting elements (`<script>` and `<template>`).
list.description: Screen readers have a specific way of announcing lists. Ensuring proper list structure aids screen reader output. [Learn more](https://web.dev/list/).

listitem.title: List items (`<li>`) are contained within `<ul>` or `<ol>` parent elements
listitem.description: Screen readers require list items (`<li>`) to be contained within a parent `<ul>` or `<ol>` to be announced properly. [Learn more](https://web.dev/listitem/).

meta-refresh.title: The document does not use `<meta http-equiv="refresh">`
meta-refresh.description: Users do not expect a page to refresh automatically, and doing so will move focus back to the top of the page. This may create a frustrating or confusing experience. [Learn more](https://web.dev/meta-refresh/).

meta-viewport.title: `[user-scalable="no"]` is not used in the `<meta name="viewport">` element and the `[maximum-scale]` attribute is not less than 5.
meta-viewport.description: Disabling zooming is problematic for users with low vision who rely on screen magnification to properly see the contents of a web page. [Learn more](https://web.dev/meta-viewport/).

object-alt.title: `<object>` elements have alternate text
object-alt.description: Screen readers cannot translate non-text content. Adding alternate text to `<object>` elements helps screen readers convey meaning to users. [Learn more](https://web.dev/object-alt/).

tabindex.title: No element has a `[tabindex]` value greater than 0
tabindex.description: A value greater than 0 implies an explicit navigation ordering. Although technically valid, this often creates frustrating experiences for users who rely on assistive technologies. [Learn more](https://web.dev/tabindex/).

td-headers-attr.title: Cells in a `<table>` element that use the `[headers]` attribute refer to table cells within the same table.
td-headers-attr.description: Screen readers have features to make navigating tables easier. Ensuring `<td>` cells using the `[headers]` attribute only refer to other cells in the same table may improve the experience for screen reader users. [Learn more](https://web.dev/td-headers-attr/).

th-has-data-cells.title: `<th>` elements and elements with `[role="columnheader"/"rowheader"]` have data cells they describe.
th-has-data-cells.description: Screen readers have features to make navigating tables easier. Ensuring table headers always refer to some set of cells may improve the experience for screen reader users. [Learn more](https://web.dev/th-has-data-cells/).

valid-lang.title: `[lang]` attributes have a valid value
valid-lang.description: Specifying a valid [BCP 47 language](https://www.w3.org/International/questions/qa-choosing-language-tags#question) on elements helps ensure that text is pronounced correctly by a screen reader. [Learn more](https://web.dev/valid-lang/).

video-caption.title: `<video>` elements contain a `<track>` element with `[kind="captions"]`
video-caption.description: When a video provides a caption it is easier for deaf and hearing impaired users to access its information. [Learn more](https://web.dev/video-caption/).

custom-controls-labels.title: Custom controls have associated labels
custom-controls-labels.description: Custom interactive controls have associated labels, provided by aria-label or aria-labelledby. [Learn more](https://web.dev/custom-controls-labels/).

custom-controls-roles.title: Custom controls have ARIA roles
custom-controls-roles.description: Custom interactive controls have appropriate ARIA roles. [Learn more](https://web.dev/custom-control-roles/).

focus-traps.title: User focus is not accidentally trapped in a region
focus-traps.description: A user can tab into and out of any control or region without accidentally trapping their focus. [Learn more](https://web.dev/focus-traps/).

focusable-controls.title: Interactive controls are keyboard focusable
focusable-controls.description: Custom interactive controls are keyboard focusable and display a focus indicator. [Learn more](https://web.dev/focusable-controls/).

interactive-element-affordance.title: Interactive elements indicate their purpose and state
interactive-element-affordance.description: Interactive elements, such as links and buttons, should indicate their state and be distinguishable from non-interactive elements. [Learn more](https://web.dev/interactive-element-affordance/).

logical-tab-order.title: The page has a logical tab order
logical-tab-order.description: Tabbing through the page follows the visual layout. Users cannot focus elements that are offscreen. [Learn more](https://web.dev/logical-tab-order/).

managed-focus.title: The user's focus is directed to new content added to the page
managed-focus.description: If new content, such as a dialog, is added to the page, the user's focus is directed to it. [Learn more](https://web.dev/managed-focus/).

offscreen-content-hidden.title: Offscreen content is hidden from assistive technology
offscreen-content-hidden.description: Offscreen content is hidden with display: none or aria-hidden=true. [Learn more](https://web.dev/offscreen-content-hidden/).

use-landmarks.title: HTML5 landmark elements are used to improve navigation
use-landmarks.description: Landmark elements (<main>, <nav>, etc.) are used to improve the keyboard navigation of the page for assistive technology. [Learn more](https://web.dev/use-landmarks/).

visual-order-follows-dom.title: Visual order on the page follows DOM order
visual-order-follows-dom.description: DOM order matches the visual order, improving navigation for assistive technology. [Learn more](https://web.dev/visual-order-follows-dom/).

uses-long-cache-ttl.title: Serve static assets with an efficient cache policy
uses-long-cache-ttl.description: A long cache lifetime can speed up repeat visits to your page. [Learn more](https://web.dev/uses-long-cache-ttl/).

total-byte-weight.title: Avoid enormous network payloads
total-byte-weight.description: Large network payloads cost users real money and are highly correlated with long load times. [Learn more](https://web.dev/total-byte-weight/).

offscreen-images.title: Defer offscreen images
offscreen-images.description: Consider lazy-loading offscreen and hidden images after all critical resources have finished loading to lower time to interactive. [Learn more](https://web.dev/offscreen-images/).

render-blocking-resources.title: Eliminate render-blocking resources
render-blocking-resources.description: Resources are blocking the first paint of your page. Consider delivering critical JS/CSS inline and deferring all non-critical JS/styles. [Learn more](https://web.dev/render-blocking-resources/).

unminified-css.title: Minify CSS
unminified-css.description: Minifying CSS files can reduce network payload sizes. [Learn more](https://web.dev/unminified-css/).

unminified-javascript.title: Minify JavaScript
unminified-javascript.description: Minifying JavaScript files can reduce payload sizes and script parse time. [Learn more](https://web.dev/unminified-javascript/).

unused-css-rules.title: Reduce unused CSS
unused-css-rules.description: Reduce unused rules from stylesheets and defer CSS not used for above-the-fold content to decrease bytes consumed by network activity. [Learn more](https://web.dev/unused-css-rules/).

unused-javascript.title: Reduce unused JavaScript
unused-javascript.description: Reduce unused JavaScript and defer loading scripts until they are required to decrease bytes consumed by network activity. [Learn more](https://web.dev/unused-javascript/).

modern-image-formats.title: Serve images in next-gen formats
modern-image-formats.description: Image formats like WebP and AVIF often provide better compression than PNG or JPEG, which means faster downloads and less data consumption. [Learn more](https://web.dev/uses-webp-images/).

uses-optimized-images.title: Efficiently encode images
uses-optimized-images.description: Optimized images load faster and consume less cellular data. [Learn more](https://web.dev/uses-optimized-images/).

node.nodeLabel: Orsogna onora quattro ebrei trucidati nel 1944

uses-text-compression.title: Enable text compression
uses-text-compression.description: Text-based resources should be served with compression (gzip, deflate or brotli) to minimize total network bytes. [Learn more](https://web.dev/uses-text-compression/).

uses-responsive-images.title: Properly size images
uses-responsive-images.description: Serve images that are appropriately-sized to save cellular data and improve load time. [Learn more](https://web.dev/uses-responsive-images/).

node.nodeLabel: Orsogna onora quattro ebrei trucidati nel 1944

efficient-animated-content.title: Use video formats for animated content
efficient-animated-content.description: Large GIFs are inefficient for delivering animated content. Consider using MPEG4/WebM videos for animations and PNG/WebP for static images instead of GIF to save network bytes. [Learn more](https://web.dev/efficient-animated-content/)

duplicated-javascript.title: Remove duplicate modules in JavaScript bundles
duplicated-javascript.description: Remove large, duplicate JavaScript modules from bundles to reduce unnecessary bytes consumed by network activity.

legacy-javascript.title: Avoid serving legacy JavaScript to modern browsers
legacy-javascript.description: Polyfills and transforms enable legacy browsers to use new JavaScript features. However, many aren't necessary for modern browsers. For your bundled JavaScript, adopt a modern script deployment strategy using module/nomodule feature detection to reduce the amount of code shipped to modern browsers, while retaining support for legacy browsers. [Learn More](https://philipwalton.com/articles/deploying-es2015-code-in-production-today/)

doctype.title: Page lacks the HTML doctype, thus triggering quirks-mode
doctype.description: Specifying a doctype prevents the browser from switching to quirks-mode. [Learn more](https://web.dev/doctype/).
doctype.explanation: Expected publicId to be an empty string

charset.title: Properly defines charset
charset.description: A character encoding declaration is required. It can be done with a `<meta>` tag in the first 1024 bytes of the HTML or in the Content-Type HTTP response header. [Learn more](https://web.dev/charset/).

dom-size.title: Avoids an excessive DOM size
dom-size.description: A large DOM will increase memory usage, cause longer [style calculations](https://developers.google.com/web/fundamentals/performance/rendering/reduce-the-scope-and-complexity-of-style-calculations), and produce costly [layout reflows](https://developers.google.com/speed/articles/reflow). [Learn more](https://web.dev/dom-size/).

Pubblicato Giovedì, 20 …

geolocation-on-start.title: Avoids requesting the geolocation permission on page load
geolocation-on-start.description: Users are mistrustful of or confused by sites that request their location without context. Consider tying the request to a user action instead. [Learn more](https://web.dev/geolocation-on-start/).

inspector-issues.title: Issues were logged in the `Issues` panel in Chrome Devtools
inspector-issues.description: Issues logged to the `Issues` panel in Chrome Devtools indicate unresolved problems. They can come from network request failures, insufficient security controls, and other browser concerns. Open up the Issues panel in Chrome DevTools for more details on each issue.

no-document-write.title: Avoid `document.write()`
no-document-write.description: For users on slow connections, external scripts dynamically injected via `document.write()` can delay page load by tens of seconds. [Learn more](https://web.dev/no-document-write/).

no-vulnerable-libraries.title: Avoids front-end JavaScript libraries with known security vulnerabilities
no-vulnerable-libraries.description: Some third-party scripts may contain known security vulnerabilities that are easily identified and exploited by attackers. [Learn more](https://web.dev/no-vulnerable-libraries/).

js-libraries.title: Detected JavaScript libraries
js-libraries.description: All front-end JavaScript libraries detected on the page. [Learn more](https://web.dev/js-libraries/).

items.name: Joomla
items.version: Joomla! 1.7 - Open Source Content Management - Versione FAP

stacks.version: Joomla! 1.7 - Open Source Content Management - Versione FAP

notification-on-start.title: Avoids requesting the notification permission on page load
notification-on-start.description: Users are mistrustful of or confused by sites that request to send notifications without context. Consider tying the request to user gestures instead. [Learn more](https://web.dev/notification-on-start/).

password-inputs-can-be-pasted-into.title: Allows users to paste into password fields
password-inputs-can-be-pasted-into.description: Preventing password pasting undermines good security policy. [Learn more](https://web.dev/password-inputs-can-be-pasted-into/).

uses-http2.title: Use HTTP/2
uses-http2.description: HTTP/2 offers many benefits over HTTP/1.1, including binary headers and multiplexing. [Learn more](https://web.dev/uses-http2/).

uses-passive-event-listeners.title: Uses passive listeners to improve scrolling performance
uses-passive-event-listeners.description: Consider marking your touch and wheel event listeners as `passive` to improve your page's scroll performance. [Learn more](https://web.dev/uses-passive-event-listeners/).

meta-description.title: Document does not have a meta description
meta-description.description: Meta descriptions may be included in search results to concisely summarize page content. [Learn more](https://web.dev/meta-description/).

http-status-code.title: Page has successful HTTP status code
http-status-code.description: Pages with unsuccessful HTTP status codes may not be indexed properly. [Learn more](https://web.dev/http-status-code/).

font-size.title: Document doesn't use legible font sizes
font-size.description: Font sizes less than 12px are too small to be legible and require mobile visitors to “pinch to zoom” in order to read. Strive to have >60% of page text ≥12px. [Learn more](https://web.dev/font-size/).
font-size.explanation: Text is illegible because there's no viewport meta tag optimized for mobile screens.

link-text.title: Links have descriptive text
link-text.description: Descriptive link text helps search engines understand your content. [Learn more](https://web.dev/link-text/).

crawlable-anchors.title: Links are crawlable
crawlable-anchors.description: Search engines may use `href` attributes on links to crawl websites. Ensure that the `href` attribute of anchor elements links to an appropriate destination, so more pages of the site can be discovered. [Learn More](https://support.google.com/webmasters/answer/9112205)

is-crawlable.title: Page isn’t blocked from indexing
is-crawlable.description: Search engines are unable to include your pages in search results if they don't have permission to crawl them. [Learn more](https://web.dev/is-crawable/).

robots-txt.title: robots.txt is valid
robots-txt.description: If your robots.txt file is malformed, crawlers may not be able to understand how you want your website to be crawled or indexed. [Learn more](https://web.dev/robots-txt/).

tap-targets.title: Tap targets are not sized appropriately
tap-targets.description: Interactive elements like buttons and links should be large enough (48x48px), and have enough space around them, to be easy enough to tap without overlapping onto other elements. [Learn more](https://web.dev/tap-targets/).
tap-targets.explanation: Tap targets are too small because there's no viewport meta tag optimized for mobile screens

hreflang.title: Document has a valid `hreflang`
hreflang.description: hreflang links tell search engines what version of a page they should list in search results for a given language or region. [Learn more](https://web.dev/hreflang/).

plugins.title: Document avoids plugins
plugins.description: Search engines can't index plugin content, and many devices restrict plugins or don't support them. [Learn more](https://web.dev/plugins/).

canonical.title: Document has a valid `rel=canonical`
canonical.description: Canonical links suggest which URL to show in search results. [Learn more](https://web.dev/canonical/).

structured-data.title: Structured data is valid
structured-data.description: Run the [Structured Data Testing Tool](https://search.google.com/structured-data/testing-tool/) and the [Structured Data Linter](http://linter.structured-data.org/) to validate structured data. [Learn more](https://web.dev/structured-data/).

configSettings.emulatedUserAgent: Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 7.0; Moto G (4)) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/98.0.4695.0 Mobile Safari/537.36 Chrome-Lighthouse

accessibility.title: Accessibility
accessibility.description: These checks highlight opportunities to [improve the accessibility of your web app](https://web.dev/lighthouse-accessibility/). Only a subset of accessibility issues can be automatically detected so manual testing is also encouraged.
accessibility.manualDescription: These items address areas which an automated testing tool cannot cover. Learn more in our guide on [conducting an accessibility review](https://developers.google.com/web/fundamentals/accessibility/how-to-review).

seo.title: SEO
seo.description: These checks ensure that your page is following basic search engine optimization advice. There are many additional factors Lighthouse does not score here that may affect your search ranking, including performance on [Core Web Vitals](https://web.dev/learn-web-vitals/). [Learn more](https://support.google.com/webmasters/answer/35769).
seo.manualDescription: Run these additional validators on your site to check additional SEO best practices.

pwa.title: PWA
pwa.description: These checks validate the aspects of a Progressive Web App. [Learn more](https://developers.google.com/web/progressive-web-apps/checklist).
pwa.manualDescription: These checks are required by the baseline [PWA Checklist](https://developers.google.com/web/progressive-web-apps/checklist) but are not automatically checked by Lighthouse. They do not affect your score but it's important that you verify them manually.

load-opportunities.title: Opportunities
load-opportunities.description: These suggestions can help your page load faster. They don't [directly affect](https://web.dev/performance-scoring/) the Performance score.

budgets.title: Budgets
budgets.description: Performance budgets set standards for the performance of your site.

diagnostics.title: Diagnostics
diagnostics.description: More information about the performance of your application. These numbers don't [directly affect](https://web.dev/performance-scoring/) the Performance score.

a11y-best-practices.title: Best practices
a11y-best-practices.description: These items highlight common accessibility best practices.

a11y-color-contrast.title: Contrast
a11y-color-contrast.description: These are opportunities to improve the legibility of your content.

a11y-names-labels.title: Names and labels
a11y-names-labels.description: These are opportunities to improve the semantics of the controls in your application. This may enhance the experience for users of assistive technology, like a screen reader.

a11y-navigation.title: Navigation
a11y-navigation.description: These are opportunities to improve keyboard navigation in your application.

a11y-aria.title: ARIA
a11y-aria.description: These are opportunities to improve the usage of ARIA in your application which may enhance the experience for users of assistive technology, like a screen reader.

a11y-language.title: Internationalization and localization
a11y-language.description: These are opportunities to improve the interpretation of your content by users in different locales.

a11y-audio-video.title: Audio and video
a11y-audio-video.description: These are opportunities to provide alternative content for audio and video. This may improve the experience for users with hearing or vision impairments.

a11y-tables-lists.title: Tables and lists
a11y-tables-lists.description: These are opportunities to improve the experience of reading tabular or list data using assistive technology, like a screen reader.

seo-mobile.title: Mobile Friendly
seo-mobile.description: Make sure your pages are mobile friendly so users don’t have to pinch or zoom in order to read the content pages. [Learn more](https://developers.google.com/search/mobile-sites/).

seo-content.title: Content Best Practices
seo-content.description: Format your HTML in a way that enables crawlers to better understand your app’s content.

seo-crawl.title: Crawling and Indexing
seo-crawl.description: To appear in search results, crawlers need access to your app.

descriptions.unused-css-rules: Consider reducing, or switching, the number of [Joomla extensions](https://extensions.joomla.org/) loading unused CSS in your page. To identify extensions that are adding extraneous CSS, try running [code coverage](https://developers.google.com/web/updates/2017/04/devtools-release-notes#coverage) in Chrome DevTools. You can identify the theme/plugin responsible from the URL of the stylesheet. Look out for plugins that have many stylesheets in the list which have a lot of red in code coverage. A plugin should only enqueue a stylesheet if it is actually used on the page.
descriptions.modern-image-formats: Consider using a [plugin](https://extensions.joomla.org/instant-search/?jed_live%5Bquery%5D=webp) or service that will automatically convert your uploaded images to the optimal formats.
descriptions.offscreen-images: Install a [lazy-load Joomla plugin](https://extensions.joomla.org/instant-search/?jed_live%5Bquery%5D=lazy%20loading) that provides the ability to defer any offscreen images, or switch to a template that provides that functionality. Starting with Joomla 4.0, all new images will [automatically](https://github.com/joomla/joomla-cms/pull/30748) get the `loading` attribute from the core.
descriptions.total-byte-weight: Consider showing excerpts in your article categories (e.g. via the read more link), reducing the number of articles shown on a given page, breaking your long posts into multiple pages, or using a plugin to lazy-load comments.
descriptions.render-blocking-resources: There are a number of Joomla plugins that can help you [inline critical assets](https://extensions.joomla.org/instant-search/?jed_live%5Bquery%5D=performance) or [defer less important resources](https://extensions.joomla.org/instant-search/?jed_live%5Bquery%5D=performance). Beware that optimizations provided by these plugins may break features of your templates or plugins, so you will need to test these thoroughly.
descriptions.unminified-css: A number of [Joomla extensions](https://extensions.joomla.org/instant-search/?jed_live%5Bquery%5D=performance) can speed up your site by concatenating, minifying, and compressing your css styles. There are also templates that provide this functionality.
descriptions.unminified-javascript: A number of [Joomla extensions](https://extensions.joomla.org/instant-search/?jed_live%5Bquery%5D=performance) can speed up your site by concatenating, minifying, and compressing your scripts. There are also templates that provide this functionality.
descriptions.efficient-animated-content: Consider uploading your GIF to a service which will make it available to embed as an HTML5 video.
descriptions.unused-javascript: Consider reducing, or switching, the number of [Joomla extensions](https://extensions.joomla.org/) loading unused JavaScript in your page. To identify plugins that are adding extraneous JS, try running [code coverage](https://developers.google.com/web/updates/2017/04/devtools-release-notes#coverage) in Chrome DevTools. You can identify the extension responsible from the URL of the script. Look out for extensions that have many scripts in the list which have a lot of red in code coverage. An extension should only enqueue a script if it is actually used on the page.
descriptions.uses-optimized-images: Consider using an [image optimization plugin](https://extensions.joomla.org/instant-search/?jed_live%5Bquery%5D=performance) that compresses your images while retaining quality.
descriptions.uses-text-compression: You can enable text compression by enabling Gzip Page Compression in Joomla (System > Global configuration > Server).
descriptions.uses-responsive-images: Consider using a [responsive images plugin](https://extensions.joomla.org/instant-search/?jed_live%5Bquery%5D=responsive%20images) to use responsive images in your content.
descriptions.server-response-time: Templates, extensions, and server specifications all contribute to server response time. Consider finding a more optimized template, carefully selecting an optimization extension, and/or upgrading your server.

rendererFormattedStrings.lsPerformanceCategoryDescription: [Lighthouse](https://developers.google.com/web/tools/lighthouse/) analysis of the current page on an emulated mobile network. Values are estimated and may vary.
rendererFormattedStrings.runtimeSingleLoadTooltip: This data is taken from a single page load, as opposed to field data summarizing many sessions.
rendererFormattedStrings.toplevelWarningsMessage: There were issues affecting this run of Lighthouse:
rendererFormattedStrings.varianceDisclaimer: Values are estimated and may vary. The [performance score is calculated](https://web.dev/performance-scoring/) directly from these metrics.
